title: Hybrid Retrieval, Score Calibration, and Rank Fusion · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release board compares near-miss decisions for hybrid retrieval, score calibration, and rank fusion and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, safe abstention, explicit uncertainty, and named native-model, vector-store, provider, load, security, recovery, or production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for hybrid retrieval and fusion evaluator. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, an…
objectives: Identify query slices where exact lexical and semantic dense signals complement or conflict instead of assuming hybrid superiority.
Avoid direct raw-score comparison across retrievers unless distributions and meaning are calibrated on target judgments.
Implement reciprocal rank fusion with stable identities, rank constant, duplicate handling, missing candidates, and deterministic ties.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

options.text: Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim does not survive the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary.

For diagnose the hidden failure, run one convenient Rag Hybrid Fallback Degradation query, omit corpus and version identity, relevance judgments, tenant policy, changed documents, abstention, citations, failure and recovery, then accept a plausible answer as RAG proof.

steps.title: RAG and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Rag Hybrid Fallback Degradation
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Rag Hybrid Fallback Degradation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz RAG engineering team handles case 2043 during an enterprise retrieval migration canary. Build deterministic Python evidence to fuse lexical and dense candidates without comparing uncalibrated raw scores; preserve source provenance, tenant authorization, deletion, freshness, citation offsets, and untrusted-data separation; then change tenant, language, date, and document version and reject stale or unauthorized evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Define behavior when one retriever, model, index, language analyzer, or filter path fails or becomes stale.
